title: Intensely Nutty Syrian Chickpea Stew
description: A kid-friendly Syrian dish featuring dry white wine and ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly ingredients, cooked to perfection in a tandoor-clay-pot.
instructions: Soak the chickpeas in water overnight, then drain and rinse them.
Heat the olive oil in the tandoor-clay-pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly.
Add the ground cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the soaked chickpeas, dry white wine, and 2 cups of water to the pot, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes.
Use a pressure cooker to cook the stew for an additional 10 minutes, or until the chickpeas are tender.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
